plan_summary.primary_goal: Deploy a 30-unit Containerized Dark Data Ingestor Network to recover 200+ petabytes of at-risk historical media over 10 years on a $250M budget, sustaining fleet uptime >90% and human review load <17.5%.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests the $250M budget surplus against bottom-up lifetime OpEx (annual OpEx/MIU × average active fleet × 10 years); the fleet uptime margin against the 90% operational target; the human review-load margin against the 17.5% safety-buffer; and the modular-replacement MTBF improvement margin against the 30%-over-OEM R&D commitment. Archive-partner liability acceptance, zero-legal-incident compliance, and long-term data-escrow format independence are treated as unmodelled existential gates.

key_values.label: 10-year total program budget
key_values.comment: Phased $60M + $120M + $70M envelope covering CapEx (≈30 MIUs) plus lifetime OpEx; the financing ceiling against which projected lifetime burn must clear.
key_values.source_text: Total project budget over ten years is $250 million — explicit input for total capital expenditure.

key_values.label: Program duration
key_values.comment: Full deployment horizon from initial 3 pilots in Year 2 to a 30-MIU fleet by Year 10; the OpEx multiplier in lifetime cost projections.
key_values.source_text: The full 30-unit network and comprehensive digitization targets must be achieved within a 10-year timeframe.

key_values.label: Fleet uptime target
key_values.comment: Below this fleet-wide uptime the maintenance program is judged unsuccessful and Phase 2 scaling CapEx funding is withheld.
key_values.source_text: Equipment Uptime target greater than 90 percent — capacity maintenance gate.

key_values.label: Human review-load safety buffer
key_values.comment: Total review load must stay below 17.5% to avoid surge staffing; the 15% override is supposed to absorb AI miscalibration within this buffer.
key_values.source_text: If the human review load exceeds 17.5 percent, then surge staffing must be funded.

key_values.label: Modular-replacement MTBF improvement target over OEM
key_values.comment: Modular assemblies must exceed OEM MTBF by 30% within 18 months for the Builder strategy to deliver; below this the pivot to cannibalization is forced.
key_values.source_text: Modular replacement MTBF must exceed original part MTBF by 30% within 18 months.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Average annual operating cost per Mobile Ingest Unit
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Risk 4 names a $2–3M baseline that could rise 20% (+$400–600K) per MIU due to logistics and engineer rotation; required for the lifetime burn-rate test against the $250M envelope.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Bottom-up: 14–17 staff per MIU at burdened rates + logistics + maintenance + power; cross-check against the $2–3M selected-scenario number with sensitivity to the Risk 4 escalation scenario.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Average active MIU count across the 10-year horizon
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Fleet ramps from 3 pilots in Year 2 to 30 units by Year 10; the time-weighted average determines lifetime OpEx burden against the budget.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Trapezoidal integration of the deployment schedule (3 → 30 over years 2–10); sensitivity to Phase-2 launch slip (Risk 2/3) that flattens the ramp.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Projected fleet-wide uptime under modular maintenance
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives whether the Phase 2 funding gate (90% uptime) clears at the Month-18 Go/No-Go review; Risk 1 names 3–6 month downtime per MIU as the dominant operational shock.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Reliability modeling from MIL-HDBK-217F baseline for modular parts × cannibalization-yield × MTTR projection; calibrate on first-3-pilots field data once available.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Projected total human review load under the 15% override
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Review Issue 3 sensitivity: if AI FPR runs 15% (vs assumed 10%) the override pushes total load to ~17.25%, dangerously close to the 17.5% buffer ceiling.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: AI FPR on the 10,000-segment audit set × (1 + override) + FNR-driven manual escalations; sensitivity to legal-taxonomy strictness.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved modular-replacement MTBF improvement over OEM
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Issue 1 names the in-house R&D capability as a load-bearing assumption; failure forces pivot to the fragile cannibalization-only path and Phase 2 fleet uptime collapses.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: MTBF test data on the first 5 modular prototypes vs. documented OEM rates; sensitivity to material-substitution risk and accelerated-life-test calibration.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Projected 10-year fleet OpEx burden
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Materialises the dominant operating-cost pressure on the $250M envelope; the rest of the budget (~$100–150M) must cover CapEx, parts, and contingency.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Budget surplus after projected lifetime OpEx
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive surplus leaves room for the ~30-MIU CapEx and parts inventory; negative surplus means OpEx alone exceeds the program envelope before any equipment is built.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Fleet uptime margin against the 90% target
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin unlocks the Month-18 Phase 2 scaling CapEx; negative margin triggers the technical-failure path that the Builder strategy's modular R&D is designed to prevent.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Review-load margin against the 17.5% safety buffer
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin means current staffing absorbs the AI override workload; negative margin forces surge staffing and risks the 20% bottleneck breach flagged in Risk 3.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Modular-MTBF improvement margin over OEM
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin validates the central technical bet of the Builder strategy; negative margin forces the Issue-1 pivot to cannibalization and a 50–100% MTTR increase on complex repairs.

unmodelled_gates.label: Pilot archives accept Tier-3 financial-penalty clauses and PTM staffing
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Issue 2 names this as a load-bearing assumption: without contractual liability for pre-treatment failures the reduced on-board MIU crew size is invalidated and hardware-damage costs flow back into Phase 1 OpEx.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: On-board crew must absorb pre-treatment risk; OpEx per MIU rises by the $400K/year/MIU savings the Builder strategy assumed, and the Phase 3 budget collapses.

unmodelled_gates.label: Zero legal or privacy incidents across the 10-year deployment
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: The plan's primary compliance metric is binary; a single GDPR or copyright incident invalidates the legal framework and exposes the project to claw-backs and partner termination regardless of any operational margin the model evaluates.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Loss of state-archive partnerships; possible termination of the entire pilot at the Phase 2 funding gate; reputational damage that the simulation cannot quantify but that closes the path to 200 PB.

unmodelled_gates.label: Three cloud-escrow providers maintain format independence and migration access through Year 10
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 7 names this as a Low/High exposure: cloud policy shifts could lock data into a single vendor and erase the societal value of the entire 10-year preservation effort.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Forced single-vendor migration at Year 10 with ~50% hosting cost inflation; the decentralized-escrow theory of post-project resilience fails and long-term societal access depends on a single counterparty.
